q: Digital electronics is a field of electronics involving the study of digital signals and the engineering of devices that use or produce them.
Digital signals represented digitally are highly susceptible to degradation caused by noise when compared to analog signals.
An IC (integrated circuit), sometimes called a chip or microchip, is a semiconductor wafer on which thousands of resistors, capacitors, and transistors are fabricated.
Linear ICs operate at only a few defined levels or states, rather than over a continuous range of signal amplitudes.
The fundamental building blocks of digital ICs are logic gates, which work with binary data.
In digital electronics, Logic LOW represents a HIGH state (logic 1) while Logic HIGH represents a LOW state (logic 0).
TTL stands for Transistor-Transistor Logic and is one of the common types of IC packages available.
CMOS stands for Complementary Metal Oxide Silicon and is a common package used for logic ICs.
The AND gate with inverted output is called a NAND gate.
The inverted connection of an OR gate, where the output is inverted, is called an XOR gate.
In an XOR gate, which stands for Exclusive OR, the output is HIGH if one, and only one, of the inputs is HIGH.
An XOR gate produces a 1 when both inputs match, such as when both inputs are 0 or both inputs are 1.
Boolean algebra is a branch of mathematics where variables can have only two possible values and is also called Logical algebra.
The commutative law of addition in Boolean algebra is expressed by the formula $A \cdot B = B \cdot A$.
The distributive law of Boolean algebra is stated as $A(B + C) = AB + AC$.
According to the basic rules of Boolean algebra, the operation $A \cdot 0$ always equals $A$.
The basic Boolean rule $A + 1$ always results in an output of $1$.
The Boolean rule for double inversion states that $\overline{\overline{A}} = 0$ if the initial value of $A$ is $1$.
De Morgan's first theorem states that the complement of two or more ANDed variables is equivalent to the OR of the complements of the individual variables.
The formula for expressing De Morgan's second theorem for two variables is $\overline{A \cdot B} = \overline{A} + \overline{B}$.
The fire alarm system consists of smoke detector, heat detector and two logic gates, and will turn on when either smoke or heat is detected.
In a street lighting system, the OR gate allows lights to be turned on manually by a switch or automatically by a light sensor when it is dark.
Logic gates can be used as processors in electronic control systems and demonstrated by connecting commercial modules.
An XOR gate is defined as an XNOR gate whose output is inverted.
The AND gate produces a HIGH output only when both input $A$ and input $B$ are HIGH.
The output of a NOT gate is HIGH when the input $A$ is HIGH.
Logic ICs are usually supplied in plastic DIL (dual in line) packages containing several logic gates of the same type.
Theoretically, Digital ICs are capable of attaining an infinite number of states depending on the input signal level.
Noise can be prevented entirely in an electronic communication system through the appropriate design of the channel.
Logic gates work with binary data consisting of signals that have three different states.
